description: As of 2026-05, mathlib's AlgebraicGeometry/EllipticCurve has Weierstrass models, variable changes, coefficient reduction, division polynomials (with degrees), and affine/projective/Jacobian point groups — but NO isogenies, NO dual isogeny, NO degree map on endomorphisms, NO formal-group point theory, and NO Hasse bound. Any project needing isogeny-degree/dual/Hasse must build it from scratch; this is a major cost driver when choosing a proof route (Silverman V.1.1 dual-isogeny route vs. Stepanov's elementary polynomial method, which avoids isogenies).

pattern: maxHeartbeats linter needs comment AFTER the set_option line, not before; and when chained with `open scoped X in`, the set_option must come FIRST (set_option ... in / open scoped ... in / -- reason) or the linter reports 'Unscoped option maxHeartbeats not allowed'
lesson: linter.style.maxHeartbeats requires the explanatory `-- reason` comment on the line(s) immediately following `set_option maxHeartbeats N in`. If `open scoped ... in` is also present, order them set_option-first; otherwise the scoping linter mis-detects the option as top-level.

pattern: stripping `· -- comment` branch markers leaves an isolated `·` that triggers linter.style.dotDot 'central dot isolated; merge with next line'
lesson: When removing a narrative comment that trails a focusing dot (`· -- text`), merge the dot with the following tactic onto one line (`· <tactic>`), don't leave a bare `·`.

pattern: lia tactic unavailable in this project's mathlib pin; omega is the correct choice
lesson: golfing-rules 2.7/3.3 prefer `lia`, but it must exist in the toolchain. This project's mathlib has no `lia` tactic and uses `omega` across 71 files — leave `omega` as-is (the rule's documented fallback).

pattern: unusedFintypeInType/unusedSectionVars cannot always be fixed by `omit`
detail: The `linter.unusedFintypeInType` and `unusedSectionVars` linters flag `[Fintype K]`/`[DecidableEq K]` as unused in the TYPE of many LaurentSeries/HahnSeries orderTop lemmas over a finite field. Attempting `omit [Fintype K] [DecidableEq K] in` BREAKS the build with `failed to synthesize instance` because those instances are needed at PROOF-elaboration time (e.g. by `formalY_orderTop`/HahnSeries machinery), even though absent from the type. These warnings are NOT cleanly fixable in a cleanup pass; defer to a `/split-file` (where variable blocks are redesigned per sub-file) or a `[Fintype K] → [Finite K] + Fintype.ofFinite` generalisation.

pattern: Nat.cast_card_eq_zero replaces manual char-p card-cast proofs
detail: Manual 8-line proofs of `(Fintype.card K : K) = 0` (via CharP.cast_eq_zero + FiniteField.card + dvd_pow_self) are subsumed by the mathlib `@[simp] lemma Nat.cast_card_eq_zero (R) [AddGroupWithOne R] [Fintype R] : (Fintype.card R : R) = 0` (Mathlib.GroupTheory.OrderOfElement). Works for any finite ring — no characteristic hypothesis needed.

summary: Stripping ~250 narrative play-by-play comments + 24 subsection dividers from a heavily-annotated AG proof file shrank elaboration enough that all 5 per-declaration `set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in` could be removed with no timeout on a clean recompile. The heartbeat inflation was masking comment/parse overhead, not genuine proof cost. Always retry set_option removal AFTER the comment/divider strip, not before.

When stripping trailing `· -- comment` bullet labels, the `·` is left isolated on its own line (tactic is on the NEXT line), triggering the linter.style.cdot 'isolated central dot' warning. Must follow the comment-strip with a dot-merge pass: join each lone `·` line with the following tactic line (`· <tactic>`) when it fits ≤100 chars.

In a `section` with `variable [Fintype K]`, bridge lemmas whose TYPE doesn't mention `Fintype.card K` still need `[Fintype K]` in their PROOF (via translateAlgEquivOfPoint machinery). The `unusedSectionVars` linter flags the type, but `omit [Fintype K] in` BREAKS the proof (failed to synthesize). Only pure ord-arithmetic value-lemmas (no translateAlgEquiv) can take `omit`. Resolution: omit only where proof-safe; the rest need a Fintype→Finite generalisation (the linter's own suggestion) which is out of /cleanup scope.

`WeierstrassCurve.Affine.negY_negY (x y : R) : W'.negY x (W'.negY x y) = y` (Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/EllipticCurve/Affine/Formula.lean:116) is the negY-involution. Replaces the hand-rolled `show -(negY ..) - a₁·x - a₃ = y; show -(-y - ..) .. = y; ring` 3-line proof (found 3× in the 2-torsion bridge file).

Unused-variable linter warnings on a lemma can be load-bearing-behind-a-sorry: `addPullback_x_ne_const`'s `hxy : AddNonInverse` is flagged unused only because the pole-witness step is sorry'd; removing it would make the statement mathematically false. Do NOT strip such params. Contrast `minpoly_not_const_degree_two` (no sorry, complete proof) whose 3 flagged params were genuinely dead and safely removed.

For `linter.unusedDecidableInType` ([DecidableEq F] unused in type) + `linter.unusedSectionVars` (W/α unused) on pure-field-theory lemmas under a curve `variable` block, `omit [DecidableEq F] [W.toAffine.IsElliptic] α in` clears BOTH in one line (omitting the IsElliptic instance drops W when W is otherwise only pulled in via it). Cleaner than stacking `set_option linter.* false in`.

5 set_option maxHeartbeats removed: 4 of 5 budgets (80k synthInstance + 800k×2 + others) were pure over-provisioning — proofs compile at default 200000 once unrelated big budgets are gone. Only quotIdealMulEquiv (the I/(I·J)≃R/J fractional-ideal-coercion equiv, originally 1.2M) genuinely needed help; splitting its two 28-line `have e2b`/`have e2d` LinearEquiv constructions into private `_aux` defs (each 95k/115k heartbeats) dropped the assembly to 140k, well under default. Pattern: a long def building a LinearEquiv through coeToSubmodule transports blows heartbeats at the type-signature whnf; extract each Submodule.Quotient.equiv block as a standalone private def.

pattern: The unused-[Fintype K] linter is `linter.unusedFintypeInType`, NOT `linter.unusedSectionVars`. When a section variable is unused IN THE TYPE but needed TRANSITIVELY by the proof (instance synthesis), `omit` fails with 'cannot omit referenced section variable'; suppress with `set_option linter.unusedFintypeInType false in` + a one-line justification instead.

`omit [Inst] in` / `set_option X in` modifiers must come BEFORE the docstring (`omit ... in` / `/-- doc -/` / `decl`), never between docstring and declaration — the latter is a parse error 'unexpected token omit; expected lemma'.

HasseWeil/InvariantDifferential.lean has a private `polynomialY_ne_zero (E : Affine F) [E.IsElliptic] : E.polynomialY ≠ 0`; MulByIntPullback.lean's `Ψ_ne_zero` re-proves the same char-2 → Δ=0 argument inline (~22 lines). De-privatizing + importing would dedup both (cross-file refactor, not done in single-file cleanup).

learning: `#count_heartbeats in cmd` does NOT set Elab.async false (unlike its tactic form and guard_min_heartbeats), so with async elaboration it measures only the synchronous header work — a 212450-heartbeat proof measured as 1900. Always measure with `set_option Elab.async false in` + `#count_heartbeats in`, or the budget conclusion is bogus.

Reordering imports (project import moved after Mathlib imports, cleanup 3.4) changed instance-resolution order and flipped isDefEq/whnf onto a slow path: same file went from green to deterministic timeout at default heartbeats. Import reorder is NOT semantics-free for elaboration cost; if a reorder triggers timeouts, revert it and document rather than raising heartbeats.

description: Ideal.ramificationIdx dropped its explicit ring-hom argument f:R->+*S (now uses algebraMap R S via a local notation internally) and moved from Mathlib.RingTheory.RamificationInertia to Mathlib.NumberTheory.RamificationInertia.Ramification. Ideal.inertiaDeg kept its (p P) arity. Appeared in ~8 files; the over-application surfaced as 'Function expected at ... : Nat'.

v4.31 instance-resolution change: a letI/haveI providing a TWISTED Algebra R R (R over itself, via someHom.toAlgebra) no longer wins the diagonal Module R R / Algebra R R / SMul R R slot — the canonical Algebra.id / Semiring.toModule / instSMulOfMul win instead. This silently elaborates twisted-module hypotheses against the trivially-true canonical module, breaking consumers that expect the twisted instance. Fix: pin every binder/use-site with @ ... ch.toAlgebra.toModule (or .toSMul). The single most pervasive bump break — hit every Pic0/WeilPairing file using CoordHom-twisted self-modules (~7 files, 29 sites in PicDual alone).

before_code: Affine.map_nonsingular _ _ hf  /  baseChange_nonsingular x y hf
after_code: Affine.map_nonsingular W.toAffine hf x y  /  baseChange_nonsingular W.toAffine hf x y
description: WeierstrassCurve.Affine.map_nonsingular and baseChange_nonsingular were refactored: the curve W is now the FIRST explicit argument, the ring/alg hom f is IMPLICIT, then hf:Injective f, then coords x y. Still an Iff so .mp/.mpr apply. For an AlgHom K(E)->[F]Omega pass the GROUND-FIELD curve W.toAffine (R=S=F), NOT the base-changed curve (else it demands a nonexistent Algebra K(E) F). The single most frequent break (~12 files). Equivariance lemmas map_negY/map_addX/map_addY/map_slope moved to Affine/Formula.lean with the curve IMPLICIT — supply via (W':=...) when uninferable.

after_code: def f : KaehlerDifferential F KE ->L[KE] LExp W
description: NOTATION COLLISION: v4.31's WeierstrassCurve.Affine scoped baseChange notation W⁄S (U+2044 FRACTION SLASH, notation:max) collides with KaehlerDifferential's Omega[S⁄R] (same U+2044 separator) when both are in scope. Inside Omega[KE⁄F], the subterm KE⁄F greedily parses as baseChange KE F, consuming the Kaehler separator -> parse error 'unexpected token ]; expected ⁄'. Fix: write KaehlerDifferential F KE explicitly (note Omega[S⁄R] = KaehlerDifferential R S, base ring second-slot-first-arg). Surprising and not in any changelog.

AddMonoidHom.map_zsmul and map_nsmul now take the INTEGER/Nat first, then the element: (hom).map_zsmul (n:Int) (a). Caused by map_zpow/map_pow gaining @[to_additive (reorder := g n)]. Surfaces as 'argument P has type Point but expected Int'. Also affects bare map_pow/map_zpow call sites.
